mechanism_narrative: Rising monthly employment gains reduce recession risk premiums, leading to positive equity returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative or flat in >60% of months where PAYEMS growth exceeds 150k, with Rank IC < 0.05 over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5%, borrowing costs compress corporate margins and discount future cash flows, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60d returns remain positive in >50% of instances where FEDFUNDS crosses above 4.5%, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 55% over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of rising unemployment and stagnant industrial production signals a supply-demand mismatch that triggers defensive positioning, yielding negative SPX returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined UNRATE-INDPRO signal fails to predict negative 5d returns in more than 40% of historical instances, or the interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a rolling OLS regression.

mechanism_narrative: During a housing market recovery phase, rising housing starts signal improving construction sector cash flows and wealth effects, boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST increases fail to generate positive 5d SPX returns in more than 50% of observed episodes, or the signal's information ratio falls below 0.2 over a 3-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate reading above 6.5% triggers recession fears that compress equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d forward returns are positive in >55% of months where UNRATE breaches 6.5%, or the signal's hit rate drops below 50% over any 4-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production declines while consumer credit remains elevated, inventory drawdowns outpace demand, signaling a supply-demand imbalance that drives negative equity returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint INDPRO-RSAFS divergence fails to predict negative 20d returns in more than 45% of historical instances, or the interaction term's predictive power vanishes (Rank IC < 0.02) over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month CPI increases erode real corporate margins and force tighter financial conditions, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL month-over-month increases do not correspond to negative 5d SPX returns in more than 50% of observations, or the signal's directional accuracy remains at chance levels over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery regime, quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.5% annualized rate signals robust earnings momentum, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth above 2.5% fails to produce positive 60d SPX returns in more than 40% of historical quarters, or the signal's forward correlation drops below 0.1 over a 5-year rolling window.
